utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check the final plan: it includes health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. Next, verify each is fully specified with all details per the format: - health_coverage: requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan provides both physical and mental entries with plan names and explicit monthly costs. Fully specified. - property_coverage: requires home/contents insurance with costs. The plan lists home_contents with a plan name and monthly cost. This satisfies home/contents insurance with costs. Fully specified. - travel_coverage: requires travel insurance with costs. The plan lists a travel plan with a monthly cost. Fully specified. - additional_services: requires any enhancement services with costs. The plan lists two services, each with plan name and monthly cost. Fully specified. - total_budget: requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. The plan provides monthly figures, including a total monthly outlay and a combined-with-benefits monthly statement, but no annual breakdown. Since the requirement asks for complete monthly/annual cost breakdown, the absence of annual cost means this item is not fully specified. Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes the following items that match entries in the ground-truth ratings: PrimeCare Premium Plan (physical health), LifeFirst Standard Plan (mental health), LifeFirst Standard Coverage (home contents), PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel insurance), Adventure Travel Coverage (travel enhancement), and Home Office Protection (property enhancement). I mapped each selected item to its exact corresponding rating number from the ground-truth list.
